name: National Credit Fixers
description_short: Credit repair company using dispute letters and FCRA compliance to challenge inaccurate items on credit reports. Operating since 1996 with BBB A+ accreditation.
description_long:
National Credit Fixers has operated as a credit repair company since 1996, positioning itself as a personalized alternative to automated credit repair services. The company is BBB-accredited with an A+ rating and claims over 20,000 Facebook followers. They emphasize human expertise over computerized systems, employing certified FICO professionals rather than call-center telemarketers.

The company offers credit repair services centered on disputing inaccurate, incomplete, outdated, or unverifiable negative items on credit reports. Their approach relies on the Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA), a federal law requiring that disputed information be 100% accurate and 100% verifiable to remain on credit reports. According to their website, they have removed bankruptcies, foreclosures, repossessions, late payments, charge-offs, collection accounts, judgments, and tax liens. They also provide educational content on credit scores, credit building, and specific topics like authorized user accounts, bankruptcy, foreclosure, and collection account strategies.

National Credit Fixers differentiates itself by claiming personalized analysis rather than automated dispute generation. They state that certified FICO professionals analyze individual credit reports and draft unique dispute letters tailored to each client's situation, addressing credit bureaus, creditors, collection agencies, and courthouses as needed. The company emphasizes direct contact with trained specialists rather than pre-scripted customer service representatives. They market themselves as "the Best Credit Repair Company" and emphasize that no two credit reports are identical, requiring individualized strategies.

The company's core claim rests on FCRA compliance and the assertion that approximately 80% of credit reports contain errors. However, as with all credit repair companies, results are not guaranteed, and the FCRA dispute process has inherent limitations. Credit repair outcomes depend heavily on whether items are actually inaccurate or unverifiable—not all negative items can be legitimately removed. Consumers should understand that this service is distinct from credit building or legitimate credit improvement, and that dispute success rates vary.\n\nIn the broader ecosystem of credit repair services, consumers have multiple paths to improving their credit. Professional credit repair companies can dispute inaccurate items with all three bureaus, while credit monitoring services provide ongoing alerts about changes to your reports. For those building credit from scratch, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured approaches. Consumers dealing with overwhelming debt may benefit from debt consolidation loans to simplify payments, or credit counseling through nonprofit agencies for personalized budgeting guidance. Consumers who successfully repair their credit often find better rates on installment loans, secured credit cards, and other financial products.

pricing.guarantee_details: 5-day cancellation window after signing. Full 100% money-back guarantee if National Credit Fixers cannot remove at least 25% of negative items within 6 months of the contract date.

tiers.name: Pay-Per-Item
features: Starts at $45/mo for 1 negative item, scales to $199/mo for 8+ items
Custom dispute letters to all three credit bureaus
Disputes with creditors and collections agencies
FDCPA violation identification
Free initial credit consultation
No setup fee for standard credit repair items

tiers.name: 12-Month Program
features: Fixed $149/month rate for structured 12-month engagement
Custom dispute letters to bureaus, creditors, collections, and courthouses
Dispute of bankruptcies, foreclosures, late payments, and charge-offs
FDCPA violation identification and recovery assistance
Legal representation for debt collector harassment
Credit education resources
Post-repair credit building guidance

tiers.name: 6-Month Accelerated
features: Accelerated 6-month dispute cycle at $199/month
Custom dispute letters to bureaus, creditors, collections, and courthouses
Dispute of bankruptcies, foreclosures, late payments, and charge-offs
FDCPA violation identification and recovery assistance
Legal representation for collector harassment claims
$199 setup fee applies for non-credit-repair items
100% money-back guarantee if fewer than 25% of items removed

pros: BBB A+ accredited business, suggesting established reputation and complaint handling
Operating since 1996, indicating long-term market presence and experience
Claims personalized analysis by certified FICO professionals rather than automated letters
Comprehensive educational resources on credit repair mechanics and FCRA rights
Targets multiple negative items: bankruptcies, foreclosures, collections, judgments, tax liens
Multiple contact methods: toll-free phone (888-623-4937), alternative number (860-282-6181), and live chat
Offers specialized programs including foreclosure deletion and business credit services
cons: Results depend entirely on whether negative items are actually inaccurate or unverifiable—not all can be removed
No specific pricing information provided on website; requires direct contact for quotes
Claims about '80% of credit reports containing errors' and '20,000 Facebook fans' are used as marketing but don't guarantee service quality
Website lacks independent verification of success rates, testimonial numbers, or average credit score improvements
Credit repair outcomes are inherently uncertain and timeline-dependent on credit bureaus' dispute processes
best_for: Consumers with verifiably inaccurate or outdated negative items on credit reports seeking FCRA-based dispute assistance
Individuals wanting human-based analysis over fully automated credit repair services
People dealing with multiple negative items (collections, judgments, foreclosures) who want coordinated dispute strategy
diagnosis: National Credit Fixers is appropriate for consumers with genuinely inaccurate, incomplete, or unverifiable negative items on their credit reports who prefer human-based dispute strategy over fully automated services. The main caveat is that credit repair can only remove items that are actually inaccurate or unverifiable under FCRA—legitimate negative items cannot be removed, and results are never guaranteed.
services: Credit repair disputes targeting inaccurate items
Certified mail dispute letter preparation and submission
Tax lien and judgment removal services
typical_results_timeline: Initial dispute responses from bureaus typically arrive within 30–45 days of the first round of letters. The company's own guarantee is structured around a 6-month window, with meaningful removal progress expected by that point. Clients reporting positive outcomes cite score gains of 60–110+ points over 3–6 months.
